comments.text:
## Design Review Rationale — 2026-07-15

This child was deepened around one principle: callers should learn App Settings behavior, not storage mechanics.

The versioned envelope is intentionally backend-only. The frontend needs the current settings, an optional load warning, and a typed update result; it does not need to know which schema version was serialized or how Tauri Store resolves its path. This keeps migrations/defaulting/repair local and prevents every future settings control from branching on persistence details.

A dedicated Settings store is not needless duplication with Workspace Catalog. The two modules differ in ownership and lifecycle: Workspace Catalog is tied to Current Workspace restoration and recovery; App Settings must load with no Workspace, survive every Workspace transition unchanged, and be independently reset/tested. Separate `BEADSMITH_SETTINGS_STORE_PATH` isolation also prevents a settings E2E scenario from accidentally coupling to Workspace fixtures.

The load interface returns usable defaults plus a typed warning rather than failing the whole app. Missing state is normal first-run behavior; malformed/unsupported/read-failed state is not. The latter must remain visible and repairable without blocking Issue browsing.

The store is not rewritten on read failure because an unsupported version may come from a newer app. Only a deliberate user update/Reset is allowed to replace it. Save success is the commit point; failed save must not advance backend confirmed state.

A complete `AppSettings` update keeps one document transaction and makes future prose/mono font additions coherent. Do not expose arbitrary key/value writes or a frontend store adapter. If the merged runtime offers a shared container, use it only as ownership plumbing; do not make Settings depend on Current Workspace initialization.

Cross-epic sequencing was added because the active Workspace integration changes the same `rpc.rs`, runtime initialization, generated bindings, and E2E infrastructure. Waiting for `bsm-kia.5` reduces rework without blocking the independent Markdown renderer task.

## Repair-Path Amendment — 2026-07-15

Added an explicit adapter requirement for malformed whole-file recovery. `tauri-plugin-store` may fail while opening an unreadable file, before normal key replacement is possible. A deliberate valid edit/Reset therefore needs a dedicated settings-file replace path analogous to Workspace memory reset fallback: remove only the App Settings file, recreate a valid version-1 document, and surface failure if that cannot be done. Passive startup still never deletes data.

## Sequencing Note — 2026-07-15

The final dependency is on the parent epic, not directly on this child: `bsm-kia.5 blocks bsm-al6`. Beadwork propagates that blocker to all children. The earlier rationale that the renderer could proceed immediately was technically true at the code level but misleading in the actual ready queue; both foundations intentionally wait, then may proceed in parallel after Workspace integration lands.

## Dependency Reassessment — 2026-07-15

This backend task has no true dependency on any open `bsm-kia` child. It needs Tauri Store registration, TauRPC generation, and an application runtime, all already present. Closed Workspace work may change the same files before this branch lands, so the implementer must base/rebase on current main, but no formal blocker is justified.

## Implementation plan

1. Add `src-tauri/src/settings.rs` module with the App Settings domain, store seam, and service:
   - `AppSettings` / `MarkdownSettings` (default 14, valid 8..72)
   - `AppSettingsState` with an optional `AppSettingsWarning`
   - `AppSettingsError` and `AppSettingsErrorKind`
   - `AppSettingsStore` trait: `load` / `save` / `replace`
   - `SettingsService` implementing load/update semantics and malformed-store repair
   - `TauriAppSettingsStore` backed by `tauri-plugin-store`, file `app-settings.json`, key `settings`, env override `BEADSMITH_SETTINGS_STORE_PATH`
   - Pure fake-store unit tests covering missing, valid (8/14/72), invalid (7/73/fractional/wrong type/missing field), malformed, unsupported schema version, store read failure, save failure, and repair-after-load-warning.

2. Extend `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`:
   - Add `app_settings_state()` and `update_app_settings(settings: AppSettings)` to `BeadsmithApi`
   - Add a `settings` field and `initialize_settings` to `BeadsmithApiImpl`
   - Implement resolvers that delegate to `SettingsService`

3. Update `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`:
   - `pub mod settings;`
   - Initialize the settings runtime after the store plugin is registered.

4. Regenerate `src/rpc/bindings.ts` through `cargo test` in `src-tauri` (TauRPC macro export).

5. Extend the `generates_typescript_bindings` test to assert the new settings types/methods.

6. Verify:
   - `cargo test` and `cargo fmt` in `src-tauri`
   - `pnpm test` and `pnpm check` / `pnpm fix`
   - generated bindings compile against TypeScript.

Out of scope for this slice: React Settings page, number input, preview, Markdown CSS, font-family fields, and E2E acceptance.

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[settings.rs module] --> B[Tauri store adapter]
    A --> C[Fake store tests]
    B --> D[SettingsService]
    D --> E[rpc.rs TauRPC methods]
    E --> F[lib.rs runtime init]
    F --> G[cargo test generates bindings]
    G --> H[TS + Rust test verification]
```

title: Persist versioned app settings through TauRPC
